err: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Erre.
ley: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemolang.
lop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lopa.
lope: verb. To travel an easy pace with long strides.
loper: noun. One who or that which lopes; a runner; a leaper.
loppy: adjective. Somewhat lop; inclined to lop; droopy; floppy
lore: noun. All the facts and traditions about a particular subject that have been accumulated over time through education or experience.
lorry: noun. A large and heavy motor vehicle designed to carry goods or soldiers; a truck
lory: noun. Any of various small, brightly coloured parrots native to Australasia.
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
lyre: noun. An ancient stringed musical instrument (a yoke lute chordophone) of Greek origin, consisting of two arms extending from a body to a
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
orle: noun. A bordure that runs around the outline of a shield without touching the edge.
oyer: noun. A hearing in a civil case which is based on the content of a document, in which the plaintiff is required to produce the document.
pep: verb. To inject with energy and enthusiasm.
pepo: noun. A fruit of plants of the gourd family Cucurbitaceae, possessing a hard rind and producing many seeds in a single, central, pulpy
per: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Persian.
perry: noun. A fermented alcoholic beverage made from pears, similar to hard apple cider.
plop: noun. A sound or action like liquid hitting a hard surface, or an object falling into a body of water.
ploy: noun. A tactic, strategy, or scheme.
ply: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bolyu.
plyer: noun. A kind of balance used in raising and letting down a drawbridge.
pol: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Polish.
pole: noun. Originally, a stick; now specifically, a long and slender piece of metal or (especially) wood, used for various construction or
poler: noun. One who propels a boat using a pole.
polyp: noun. A cylindrical coelenterate, such as the hydra, having a mouth surrounded with tentacles.
pop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pwapwâ.
pope: noun. An honorary title of the Roman Catholic bishop of Rome as father and head of his church, a sovereign of the Vatican city state.
popery: noun. The teachings, practices and accoutrements of the Roman Catholic Church.
pore: noun. A tiny opening in the skin.
prey: noun. That which is or may be seized by animals to be devoured.
pro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old Occitan.
prole: noun. A member of the proletariat; a proletarian.
prop: noun. An object placed against or under another, to support it; anything that supports.
propel: verb. To provide an impetus for motion or physical action; to cause to move in a certain direction; to drive or push forward.
properly: adverb. In a proper manner, appropriately, suitably; correctly, justifiably
propyl: noun. Either of two isomeric univalent hydrocarbon radicals, C 3 H 7, formally derived from propane by the loss of a hydrogen atom.
pry: verb. To peer closely and curiously, especially at something closed or not public.
pryer: noun. A person who pries; a pry.
pye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pye Krumen.
pyre: noun. A funeral pile; a combustible heap on which corpses are burned.
pyrope: noun. A variety of garnet, of a poppy or blood-red color, frequently with a tinge of orange.
pyrrole: noun. Any of a class of aromatic heterocyclic compounds containing a ring of four carbon atoms and a nitrogen atom; especially the simplest
rely: verb. To trust; to have confidence in; to depend.
reply: verb. To give a written or spoken response, especially to a question, request, accusation or criticism; to answer.
repo: noun. Clipping of repossession, most commonly of a vehicle, house or condominium.
roe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ronji.
role: noun. A character or part played by a performer or actor.
rope: noun. Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.
roper: noun. Agent noun of rope; one who uses a rope, especially one who throws a lariat or lasso.
ropery: noun. Any form of language used, typically slang or slander, that can get one in trouble.
ropy: adjective. Resembling rope in appearance or texture, used especially of muscles that are thick or hard to the touch.
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
yelp: noun. An abrupt, high-pitched noise or utterance.
